title: [security] "Password reset successful" banner driven by spoofable `?reset=true...
description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.ts:785
Severity: Low

Problem:
"Password reset successful" banner driven by spoofable `?reset=true` URL parameter — any visitor can navigate to `/auth/sign-in?reset=true` to display the success message, enabling a social-engineering/phishing pretext. The `registeredSuccess` case was already migrated to a flash cookie; `passwordResetSuccess` was not.

Fix:
Use a `__Host-flash_reset` cookie (same pattern as `flash_registered`) and clear it on GET /auth/sign-in.

title: Exploratory Testing
description: Systematic exploratory testing of the site to find bugs, UX issues, and broken functionality

title: [sp:07-implement] Exploratory Testing Bug Fixes
description: Parent task for all bugs found during exploratory testing

title: Homepage: placeholder text throughout hero, meta tags, and structured data
description: The homepage h1 says 'Welcome to My Hugo Site' and description says 'A modern static website built with Hugo, TailwindCSS, and DaisyUI'. This placeholder text appears in: og:title, og:description, twitter:title, twitter:description, Schema.org description, and the visible hero section. Only the <title> tag and meta description are correct.

title: Missing robots.txt: returns 404 instead of proper robots directives
description: Requesting /robots.txt returns a 404. Search engine crawlers expect this file. Should serve a robots.txt with at minimum a Sitemap directive pointing to /sitemap.xml and appropriate crawl rules.

title: Sample blog post is placeholder Hugo tutorial content
description: The only blog post (/posts/sample-post/) titled 'Getting Started with Hugo' is clearly scaffolding/template content about building sites with Hugo, TailwindCSS, and DaisyUI. It should be replaced with actual ClawTask content or removed before launch.

title: Homepage body: placeholder content 'Welcome to your new Hugo site'
description: Below the hero section, the homepage body content is a single placeholder paragraph: 'Welcome to your new Hugo site powered by TailwindCSS and DaisyUI\!' This should be replaced with actual ClawTask content describing the product.

title: About page: 'Reach out through social channels' but no social links exist
description: The About page Contact Us section says 'Reach out to us through our social channels' but there are no social media links anywhere on the site (they were previously removed as placeholders from the footer). Either add actual social links or update the contact copy with a real contact method.

title: Privacy, Terms, About: 'contact us' with no contact method provided
description: Multiple pages say 'please contact us' or 'reach out through our social channels' but provide no email address, contact form, or social media links. Affected pages: /privacy/ ('please contact us'), /terms/ ('please contact us'), /about/ ('Reach out to us through our social channels'). Users have no way to actually reach the company.

title: No favicon link tag in HTML head on any page
description: No <link rel='icon'> tag exists in the HTML <head> of any page (Hugo pages or auth pages). Combined with missing /favicon.ico file, browsers show a generic tab icon. Also missing: apple-touch-icon and web app manifest.

title: Sign-in: 'registered=true' query parameter is spoofable
description: Visiting /auth/sign-in?registered=true shows 'Account created successfully. Please sign in.' success banner without any actual registration. While low-risk (no data exposure), it's a UX inconsistency. The success state should be tied to a server-side session flag rather than a client-visible query parameter.

title: Rate limit page: 'Back to Sign In' link shown regardless of origin page
description: When rate-limited during sign-up, the rate limit page shows 'Back to Sign In' instead of 'Back to Sign Up'. The rate limit handler doesn't track which page triggered the limit, so the link always goes to sign-in. Should either link to the originating page or provide both links.

title: API auth: session cookie missing Secure flag from better-auth endpoints
description: The better-auth JSON API (/api/auth/sign-up/email, /api/auth/sign-in/email) sets the session cookie without the Secure attribute. The form-based auth handlers correctly set Secure via cookieBuilder.ts, but the direct API endpoint bypasses this. On HTTP connections the session cookie could be transmitted in cleartext. HSTS mitigates in production, but defense-in-depth requires Secure on the cookie itself.

title: API auth: sign-up/sign-in JSON endpoints accept cross-origin requests
description: POST to /api/auth/sign-up/email and /api/auth/sign-in/email with Origin: http://evil.com succeeds, creating real accounts. Other state-changing better-auth endpoints (sign-out, change-password, etc.) correctly check Origin and return 403. An attacker page could abuse the sign-up endpoint for automated account creation. SameSite=Lax provides partial mitigation.

title: Breadcrumb JSON-LD: Home item named 'Welcome to My Hugo Site' on all pages
description: The breadcrumb structured data (JSON-LD) on every page that has breadcrumbs uses 'Welcome to My Hugo Site' as the name for the Home item (position 2). This affects SEO and should say 'ClawTask' or 'Home'. Found on /about/, /posts/, /404.html, and likely all pages using the breadcrumb partial.

title: Sign-up form missing name field that API requires
description: The HTML sign-up form only has email, password, and _csrf fields. The /api/auth/sign-up/email endpoint requires a 'name' parameter (returns VALIDATION_ERROR without it). The form handler works around this by using the email prefix as display name, but users cannot set their name during registration.

title: HEAD request to /api/auth/get-session returns 404 instead of 200
description: GET /api/auth/get-session returns 200 with body null, but HEAD to the same URL returns 404. Per HTTP spec, HEAD should return the same status code and headers as GET, just without a body. Likely the Hono route is registered only for GET without automatic HEAD derivation. Affects caching proxies and monitoring tools.

title: Hugo generator meta tag discloses exact framework version
description: Homepage includes <meta name=generator content='Hugo 0.154.5'> revealing the exact Hugo version. While Hugo is a build-time tool (not runtime), revealing exact versions aids attackers in targeting known vulnerabilities. Should be removed or stripped of the version number in hugo.toml with disableHugoGeneratorInject.

title: Dashboard page included in sitemap.xml despite being auth-protected
description: sitemap.xml includes <url><loc>https://meetclawtask.com/dashboard/</loc></url> but the dashboard returns 302 redirect to sign-in for unauthenticated users. Auth-protected pages should not appear in the public sitemap. When crawled, the redirect may cause the sign-in page to be indexed as the dashboard content.

title: No og:image meta tag configured on any page
description: No OpenGraph image meta tag is present on any page. Social media shares (Facebook, LinkedIn, Slack, etc.) will have no image preview. The params.yaml has a STARTUPFIXME comment with instructions to add images/og-image.png (1200x630px recommended). This was identified as a known TODO but not yet addressed.

title: State change: lifecycle → in-progress
description: Set lifecycle to in-progress

title: Auth forms: inputs and labels not full-width (DaisyUI 5 layout issue)
description: On sign-in/sign-up pages, labels render inline beside inputs instead of stacked above them. DaisyUI 5's .label is display:inline-flex and .input has a fixed 320px width. Inputs occupy only ~71% of card body width. The submit button is also narrow (80px). Missing w-full class on input elements. Files: login.ts lines 64,80 and register.ts lines 84,100.

title: Sign-up: no password confirmation field (no forgot-password exists either)
description: The sign-up form has only a single password field with no confirmation. Users could typo their password and be locked out permanently since there is no password reset mechanism. Either add a confirm-password field or implement a forgot-password flow (or both).

title: No forgot-password / password-reset flow exists
description: There is no 'Forgot password?' link on the sign-in page, and /auth/forgot-password and /auth/reset-password both return 404. Users who forget their password have no recovery path. Combined with no password confirmation on sign-up, users who typo their password are permanently locked out.

title: No double-submit prevention on auth forms
description: Sign-in and sign-up forms have no client-side double-submit prevention. No onsubmit handler, no Alpine.js directives, no HTMX attributes. Rapid clicking sends multiple POST requests, which could consume rate limit attempts or create duplicate requests. Should disable submit button after first click.

title: GET /auth/sign-out returns blank white page (405 with empty body)
description: Navigating to /auth/sign-out via GET returns HTTP 405 with Content-Length:0 — a completely blank white page. Should show a user-friendly message explaining sign-out requires POST, redirect to sign-in, or show a sign-out confirmation button. Source: worker.ts line 337 returns c.body(null, 405).

title: RSS feed: title and description contain placeholder text
description: The RSS feed at /index.xml has title 'Welcome to My Hugo Site on ClawTask' and description 'A modern static website built with Hugo, TailwindCSS, and DaisyUI'. Both should reflect actual ClawTask branding and description.

title: Auth pages missing meta robots noindex tag
description: Sign-in, sign-up, and rate-limit pages have no <meta name='robots' content='noindex, nofollow'> tag. Search engines could index login/registration forms. Auth pages should be excluded from indexing. Source: authLayout.ts template.

title: Theme mismatch: Hugo pages use 'lemonade' but auth pages use 'turtlebased'
description: Static Hugo pages (homepage, about, posts, etc.) set data-theme='lemonade' while dynamic auth pages (sign-in, sign-up, rate-limit) set data-theme='turtlebased'. This creates a jarring visual inconsistency when navigating between static and dynamic pages. Both should use the same theme.

title: Auth pages: no site navigation or branding — users get trapped
description: The sign-in, sign-up, and rate-limit pages have no navbar, logo link, or any way to navigate back to the main site. Users who land on these pages can only use browser back. All other pages have the full navbar. Auth pages should include at minimum a ClawTask logo linking to home.

title: Sign-up form: no password requirements shown to user
description: The sign-up form has no indication of password requirements (minimum length, complexity, etc.). When a weak password is rejected, the user has no prior guidance on what constitutes an acceptable password. Should display password requirements near the password field.

title: 404 page: robots meta allows indexing (should be noindex)
description: The 404 page at /404.html has <meta name=robots content='index, follow'>. Search engines should not index 404 pages. Should be 'noindex, follow' or 'noindex, nofollow'. Also, og:type is 'article' when it should be 'website'.

title: 404 page: inline onclick handler violates CSP best practices
description: The 404 page 'Go Back' button uses onclick=history.back() — an inline event handler. While the current CSP includes 'unsafe-inline' for script-src on Hugo pages (needed for GA), inline handlers are a security anti-pattern. Should use addEventListener from an external script instead.

title: Missing favicon: /favicon.ico returns 404
description: Requesting /favicon.ico returns a 404 status with the full 404 HTML page. Browsers automatically request this file, so every page load generates a 404 in logs. Should serve an actual favicon or at minimum a proper 204/empty response.

title: [quality] No test for `{ ok: false, kind: 'service_error' }` from `RequestPas...
description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.spec.ts
Severity: Medium

Problem:
No test for `{ ok: false, kind: 'service_error' }` from `RequestPasswordResetUseCase` — the anti-enumeration redirect behaviour on unexpected failure is unverified.

Fix:
Add a test mocking any `ok: false` non-`rate_limited` result and asserting a 303 redirect to `?success=true`.

title: Feature: exploratory-testing
description: Exploratory testing: auth polish, security fixes, and content improvements from PR #19

title: [sp:07-implement] Implement exploratory-testing fixes
description: Implementation task for review findings from PR #19
close_reason: All 18 subtasks completed and committed: quality fixes (JSDoc, test coverage, type exports, console.info routing), security fixes (token redaction, spoofable param replacement), architecture fix (ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase), and documentation improvements.

title: fix: is_excluded() false positive on .git prefix matches .github, .gitignore, .gitattributes
description: The is_excluded() function in build-template.sh line 55 strips trailing slash and does prefix matching: [[ $path == ${exclusion%/}* ]]. For exclusion '.git/', this becomes '.git'* which incorrectly matches .github/, .gitignore, .gitattributes. Same pattern affects .beads/ matching .beadsomething if it existed. Fix: use exact directory prefix matching with trailing slash only, e.g. [[ $path == ${exclusion}* ]] without stripping the slash, or add slash to the prefix check.

title: fix: files_excluded counter lost in subshell pipe in get_files()
description: In build-template.sh, get_files() uses 'git ls-files | while read' which runs the while loop in a subshell. The files_excluded counter incremented inside the subshell (line 99) is lost when the subshell exits. The summary always reports 0 excluded files. Fix: use process substitution 'while read -r file; do ... done < <(git ls-files ...)' inside get_files, or track exclusions differently.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Bug already fixed in commit 7ac4991. get_files() already uses process substitution and temp file to track exclusion count. No code changes needed.

title: fix: output filename deviates from spec FR-002 (turtlebased-ts.tmplr.gz vs template.tmplr)
description: Spec FR-002 requires output to dist/template.tmplr. Implementation outputs dist/turtlebased-ts.tmplr.gz instead. Two deviations: (1) filename is turtlebased-ts.tmplr not template.tmplr, (2) gzip compression added (.gz suffix) which spec doesn't mention. The quickstart.md also references dist/template.tmplr. Either update spec to match implementation or fix the output path.

title: chore: scope creep - wrangler.worker.toml, cron handler, scaffold dirs not in spec
description: build-template.sh includes content not specified in the feature spec: (1) wrangler.worker.toml generation (lines 143-209), (2) example cron task handler functions/cron/example-task.ts (lines 261-321), (3) scaffold directories thoughts/handoffs and thoughts/ledgers (lines 211-215). These reference FR-014 in comments but FR-014 is about tmplr binary installation, not worker configs. Not blocking but should be documented in spec or removed to match spec scope.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting: will read build-template.sh and spec to determine what to remove

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Investigation complete: all three scope creep items (wrangler.worker.toml, cron handler, scaffold dirs) were added in commits e7e6603 and 7a42b28 but are no longer present in the current build-template.sh (231 lines). They were cleaned up during the merge commit 85531f9. No remaining references found in CI, skills, or docs. Closing as already resolved.

title: bug: Clarify button sends bare POST without required form data
description: The Clarify button on /app/inbox fires hx-post to /app/_/inbox/:id/clarify but sends NO form data. The handler expects title, areaId, and contextId from FormData. Without these, the use case fails with 422 (area_not_found_or_archived or context_not_found). The button needs to open a form/modal to collect title, area, and context before submitting. Reproduced in local dev at http://localhost:8787/app/inbox.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Fixed: Clarify button now opens a DaisyUI modal form collecting title, area, and context before POSTing. Updated template, page handler (fetches areas/contexts), DI wiring, and all tests.

title: bug: Dashboard capture form lacks hx-target, replaces itself on submit
description: The capture form on /app (dashboard) has hx-post but no hx-target or hx-swap. HTMX defaults to replacing the form's innerHTML with the response (<li> element). After capture: (1) the input field disappears so you can't capture more items, (2) the captured item renders as an unstyled bullet point outside the inbox list context. The form needs hx-target pointing to an inbox item list and hx-swap='beforeend', plus the form should reset rather than be replaced.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Fixed: added hx-target, hx-swap=beforeend, form reset, and captured-items list to dashboard capture form

title: bug: Dashboard inbox counter not updated after capture
description: After capturing an inbox item on the dashboard, the Inbox counter card still shows the old count. It only updates on full page reload. The capture partial response returns a bare <li> with no out-of-band (OOB) swap to update the counter. The capture handler should include an HX-Trigger header or OOB swap fragment to update the inbox count after successful capture.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Fixed: added HX-Trigger header to capture handler and inbox-count id with client-side increment listener on dashboard

title: bug: Clarify button has no hx-target/hx-swap, error response invisible to user
description: The Clarify button on /app/inbox has hx-post but no hx-target or hx-swap attributes. When the server returns 422 or 500, HTMX silently does nothing visible — no error message shown to the user. The button appears completely broken. Even once the form data issue (clawtask-4qj.2) is fixed, proper hx-target and error handling are needed so users see feedback.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Fixed: added error container in clarify modal, HX-Retarget/HX-Reswap headers on 422, and HTMX responseHandling config to swap on 422

title: bug: Dashboard Inbox/Actions cards are not clickable
description: The Inbox and Actions stat cards on /app dashboard are plain divs, not links. Users expect to click 'Inbox: 2' to navigate to /app/inbox and 'Actions: 0' to navigate to /app/actions. Cards should be wrapped in <a> tags or have cursor-pointer with click handlers.

title: bug: Capture and Clarify handlers crash on missing/malformed form data
description: Both handleCaptureInbox and handleClarifyInbox call c.req.formData() without guarding against missing Content-Type header (throws TypeError), and pass formData.get() results directly to domain constructors without null checks (causes TypeError on .trim() in requireNonBlank). Sentry issues #7364129886 and #7364123536. Handlers should validate form data presence and return 422 with a user-friendly error instead of crashing with 500.

title: [quality] `SignUpRequest` and `SignUpResult` are unexported type aliases
description:
File: src/application/use-cases/SignUpUseCase.ts:20,43
Severity: Medium

Problem:
`SignUpRequest` and `SignUpResult` are unexported type aliases — inconsistent with every other use-case in this PR which exports its Request/Result types.

Fix:
Add `export` keyword to both type aliases.

title: [quality] `console
description:
File: src/infrastructure/auth.ts:161
Severity: Low

Problem:
`console.info(...)` used in `sendResetPassword` stub instead of the injected Logger port — requires a `no-console` eslint-disable comment.

Fix:
Route through the injected Logger to remove the disable comment and stay consistent with the codebase's logging discipline.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: replace console.info with injected Logger

title: [security] "Email sent" banner on forgot-password driven by spoofable `?succes...
description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.ts:914
Severity: Low

Problem:
"Email sent" banner on forgot-password driven by spoofable `?success=true` URL parameter — same issue; anyone can pre-craft a link that shows the confirmation banner without submitting the form.

Fix:
Use a short-lived HttpOnly flash cookie instead of a query parameter for this redirect state.

title: [architecture] Infrastructure layer imports and instantiates `ProvisionWorkspaceUs...
description:
File: src/infrastructure/auth.ts:50,115
Severity: High

Problem:
Infrastructure layer imports and instantiates `ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase` (an application-layer use case) directly inside `getAuth()`, bypassing the DI composition root.

Fix:
Accept a `WorkspaceProvisioningService` (or a callback port) as a parameter to `getAuth()`, and pass it from `ServiceFactory` — which already manages `provisionWorkspaceUseCase` at lines 495–500 of `serviceFactory.ts`.

title: Feature: exploratory-testing
description: Exploratory testing of the clawtask web interface to identify bugs and UX issues

title: BUG: App page routes never wired in worker.ts -- entire GTD web UI returns 404
description: AppPageHandlers has handlers for /app, /app/inbox, and /app/actions, and ServiceFactory exposes appPageHandlers and appPartialHandlers, but worker.ts never registers these routes. The only /app/* route is the partial catch-all. All /app/* page requests fall through to the static asset handler, returning a blank 404.

title: BUG: Error response format inconsistencies across API endpoints
description: Four issues: (1) Action error messages duplicate the code as the message. (2) Domain errors have unhelpful messages like 'domain_error' for title_too_long. (3) 404 catch-all returns string vs standard object format. (4) better-auth validation errors use different wrapper than standard.

title: BUG: Unsupported HTTP methods return 404 instead of 405 on /api/v1/*
description: PUT, DELETE, PATCH on /api/v1/inbox return 404 with 'Not found'. Per RFC 7231, should return 405 with an Allow header. Auth routes correctly return 405 but API routes do not.

title: BUG: Huge Content-Length header causes server to hang (potential DoS)
description: Sending Content-Length: 999999999 with a small body causes the connection to time out. No request body size limit is enforced at the HTTP layer for API routes. Form-based auth handlers have MAX_BODY_BYTES=4096 but API routes do not.

title: BUG: handleGetSignIn ignores redirectTo for already-authenticated users
description: When an already-authenticated user hits /auth/sign-in?redirectTo=/dashboard/, they are redirected to / unconditionally, discarding the redirectTo parameter. Compounds with the localhost auth cookie bug.

title: BUG: Empty title on homepage; trailing pipe on other pages (.Site.Title empty)
description: The homepage has an empty <title> element. Blog listing has 'Blog Posts |' and individual posts have 'Getting Started with Hugo |' -- the Hugo title template uses .Site.Title but it is empty, leaving a dangling pipe.

title: BUG: og:image references nonexistent images/og-image.png; og:site_name empty
description: The og:image meta tag references images/og-image.png which returns 404. Social media previews show no image. The path is also relative rather than absolute. og:site_name has an empty content attribute.

title: BUG: No mobile-responsive navigation (no hamburger menu)
description: No hamburger menu, drawer, or dropdown for mobile viewports. The navbar has 7+ horizontal links that compress or overflow at phone widths. Brand logo/text area is also invisible below the lg breakpoint (1024px).

title: BUG: Footer Social section is empty placeholder
description: The footer has a 'Social' heading with no child links -- an unfilled placeholder visible on every page.

title: BUG: Rate limit page has broken text layout
description: The 'Too Many Requests' page has a layout issue where 'Please try again in N seconds.' text floats to the right side of the alert box, split across multiple lines.

title: BUG: auth-store.js hardcodes __Host-auth_status cookie name -- auth always false on localhost
description: The server correctly drops the __Host- prefix on non-HTTPS localhost (setting cookie as auth_status=1), but auth-store.js checks for __Host-auth_status=1. Result: isAuthenticated is always false on localhost. Navbar always shows Sign In, dashboardGuard always redirects away from /dashboard/.

title: BUG: API sign-out (POST /api/auth/sign-out) returns 500 and fails to invalidate session
description: POST /api/auth/sign-out returns HTTP 500 with an empty body. The session remains valid after the call. The form-based sign-out works correctly. Root cause: the route in worker.ts passes the request to better-auth, which returns a 500 response (not an exception). The try/catch only handles thrown exceptions.

title: BUG: /about/ page linked in nav but returns empty 404
description: The About page is linked in the main navigation but returns HTTP 404 with a completely empty response body.

title: BUG: /privacy/ and /terms/ pages linked in footer but return empty 404
description: Both pages are linked from the footer on every page but return 404 with empty responses. Users see a blank white page.

title: BUG: Hugo baseURL still set to example.com -- blog post links navigate away from site
description: All Hugo-generated absolute URLs point to https://example.com/. This affects navbar logo link, footer brand link, blog post links, canonical URL, and og:url. Blog post links are completely broken.

title: BUG: All 404 responses return blank pages (empty body)
description: Any non-existent URL returns HTTP 404 with Content-Length: 0 -- a blank white page. No Page Not Found message, no navigation to return to the site.

title: BUG: Malformed JSON on auth endpoints returns 500 instead of 400
description: Sending malformed JSON to /api/auth/sign-up/email or /api/auth/sign-in/email returns HTTP 500 with empty body. Should be 400 Bad Request. The inbox handler correctly returns 400, but better-auth pass-through routes crash.

title: BUG: Sign-up with invalid email shows wrong error (says password too short)
description: Signing up with an invalid email and valid password shows 'Password must be at least 12 characters' -- wrong error. Root cause: BetterAuthService.signUp() maps ALL 400 responses to weak_password. There is no invalid_email result kind.

title: [quality] The `ip` field passed to the use case is not asserted
description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.spec.ts
Severity: Low

Problem:
The `ip` field passed to the use case is not asserted — only `email` is checked in `expect.objectContaining(...)`.

Fix:
Extend the assertion to include `ip: 'unknown'`.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work

title: [quality] `handlePostResetPassword` returns HTTP 200 for `invalid_token`
description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.ts:247
Severity: Low

Problem:
`handlePostResetPassword` returns HTTP 200 for `invalid_token` — inconsistent with `handleGetResetPassword` which returns 400 for a missing token.

Fix:
Return `{ status: 400 }` for `invalid_token` results from POST.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: fix handlePostResetPassword to return 400 for invalid_token

title: [security] Password reset token logged in plaintext
description:
File: src/infrastructure/auth.ts:482
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Password reset token logged in plaintext — `console.info('[PasswordReset] Reset URL generated:', url)` writes the full reset URL (containing the one-time secret token) to application logs; anyone with log access can read it and reset any user's password.

Fix:
Log only a redacted confirmation (e.g. `'[PasswordReset] Reset email triggered for token: [REDACTED]'`) until real email delivery is wired up.

title: [quality] The two new methods `requestPasswordReset` and `resetPassword` adde...
description:
File: src/infrastructure/BetterAuthService.spec.ts:29
Severity: Critical

Problem:
The two new methods `requestPasswordReset` and `resetPassword` added in this PR have zero unit tests in `BetterAuthService.spec.ts` — all response branches (200, 400, 400+body code parsing, 429, 5xx, throw) are untested.

Fix:
Add `describe('requestPasswordReset', ...)` and `describe('resetPassword', ...)` suites covering every status branch, including `PASSWORD_TOO_SHORT`/`PASSWORD_TOO_LONG` body parsing and unparseable-body fallback.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: adding unit tests for requestPasswordReset and resetPassword

title: [quality] `service_error` result from `ResetPasswordUseCase` is untested
description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.spec.ts
Severity: High

Problem:
`service_error` result from `ResetPasswordUseCase` is untested — the handler branch that renders a general error message has no coverage.

Fix:
Add a test mocking `{ ok: false, kind: 'service_error' }` and asserting the response contains the general error message.

title: Feature: exploratory-testing
description: Exploratory testing of the ClawTask site to find bugs, UX issues, and broken functionality

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting exploratory testing of ClawTask site

title: [sp:07-implement] exploratory-testing
description: Implementation tasks for exploratory testing bugs

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: All 19 child bug tasks are closed. Closing parent implementation task.

title: bug: auth pages missing Tailwind CSS classes (size-8, items-center, input-bordered)
description: The @source directive in hugo/assets/css/styles.css points to '../../src/presentation/templates' which resolves incorrectly from hugo/assets/css/ to hugo/src/presentation/templates (non-existent). Should be '../../../src/presentation/templates' to reach the project root. Result: Tailwind purges classes only used in TypeScript auth templates (size-8, items-center, input-bordered, mt-2, mb-2). The favicon.svg renders at full viewport size instead of 32px, form inputs lack borders, and layout centering breaks on all auth pages (sign-in, sign-up, sign-out, forgot-password, reset-password, auth error).

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: fixing @source path in hugo/assets/css/styles.css

title: bug: API rate limit response uses flat string error instead of standard envelope
description: Rate limit middleware in apiAuthRateLimit.ts returns {"error":"Too many requests"} (flat string) while all other API errors use {"error":{"code":"...","message":"..."}} envelope format. API clients parsing response.error.code will get undefined when hitting rate limits.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: TDD approach to fix rate limit error envelope format

title: bug: auth page CSP blocks htmx inline styles and DaisyUI data: URI images
description: Auth pages CSP has style-src 'self' (no unsafe-inline) and img-src 'self' (no data:). This blocks htmx's injected inline style for .htmx-indicator and DaisyUI's data:image/svg+xml noise texture. Static pages correctly include 'unsafe-inline' in style-src and 'data:' in img-src. Console shows multiple CSP violation errors on every auth page load.

title: bug: primary button and link colors fail WCAG AA contrast (3.64:1 and 3.72:1)
description: White text on green btn-primary background has contrast ratio 3.64:1 (needs 4.5:1 for WCAG AA). Green link-primary text on light background has 3.72:1. Affects Get Started, Sign Up, Sign In, Create Account, Read More buttons and all primary links across the site. DaisyUI lemonade theme primary color needs adjustment.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: investigating theme primary color definition

title: bug: navbar links have no visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation
description: Nav links (Home, Posts, About, Sign In) have outline-style: none and outline-width: 0px with no box-shadow replacement when focused via keyboard. Keyboard users cannot see which link has focus. The .btn elements (ClawTask logo, Sign Up button) DO have visible focus indicators. WCAG 2.4.7 requires visible focus indicators on all interactive elements.

title: bug: Sign In nav link hidden when JavaScript is disabled (Alpine.js default state)
description: With JavaScript disabled, the Sign In link in the mobile dropdown menu is hidden because its parent LI uses Alpine.js :class binding with isAuthenticated store. The default state evaluates to hidden without Alpine initialization. Sign Up remains visible. Users without JS cannot access sign-in via navigation.

title: bug: auth page titles lack site name suffix (e.g. 'Sign In' instead of 'Sign In | ClawTask')
description: Auth page titles are 'Sign In', 'Create an Account', 'Forgot Password' without the '| ClawTask' suffix used by all Hugo pages (e.g. 'Blog Posts | ClawTask', '404 Page not found | ClawTask'). Inconsistent browser tab experience and weaker SEO signal.

title: bug: no active/current state on navigation links for the current page
description: The navbar does not highlight the current page. None of the nav links have aria-current='page' or an active CSS class. Users cannot tell which page they are on from the navigation. Accessibility and usability issue.

title: bug: reset-password page missing confirm password field and requirements hint
description: The reset-password form has only one password field with no confirm field (sign-up form has one). Also missing the 'Must be at least 12 characters' hint text that sign-up shows, though it does have minlength=12. Users could mistype new password with no way to catch it. Inconsistent UX between sign-up and reset-password.

title: bug: sign-up page has no terms of service or privacy policy links
description: The sign-up form does not reference Terms of Service or Privacy Policy. Users creating accounts should see and/or agree to terms. The footer links exist on Hugo pages but the auth pages (rendered by Worker) have no footer. For legal compliance, users should be informed of terms when creating an account.

title: bug: blog post URL slug is generic 'sample-post' instead of descriptive
description: The only blog post has URL /posts/sample-post/ derived from filename sample-post.md. The article title is 'Why Most OKRs Fail — and What to Do About It'. Slug should be descriptive like 'why-most-okrs-fail' for SEO and professionalism.

title: bug: favicon link tags use hardcoded meetclawtask.com URLs
description: In the HTML head of all Hugo-rendered pages, favicon link tags use absolute URLs (e.g. href=https://meetclawtask.com/favicon-96x96.png) instead of relative paths. While canonical and OG tags correctly use the production baseURL, favicon links should use relative paths (/favicon-96x96.png) so they work across environments. The favicons still load via browser fallback but the explicit link tags don't resolve in local dev.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: investigating favicon link tags in hugo/layouts/baseof.html

title: bug: /series/ page in sitemap but shows 'No content found'
description: The /series/ taxonomy page appears in sitemap.xml but displays 'No content found.' to users. Either add content/series or remove /series/ from the sitemap to avoid indexing an empty page.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: investigating Hugo series taxonomy and sitemap configuration

title: bug: API auth endpoints return 403 origin_not_allowed despite correct Origin header
description: POST requests to /api/auth/sign-in/email and /api/auth/sign-up/email consistently return 403 with {error: {code: 'origin_not_allowed'}} even when sending Origin: http://localhost:8787, which matches BETTER_AUTH_URL in .dev.vars. The form-based auth endpoints (/auth/sign-in, /auth/sign-up) work correctly with CSRF. Browser fetch() from same origin also gets 403. Historical logs show these endpoints DID work (200 OK) in a prior session. The isOriginAllowed() function in originCheck.ts looks correct. Possible causes: wrangler/miniflare header handling, env var loading timing, or isolate caching of stale env.

title: bug: heading hierarchy skips levels (H3→H6 footer, H3 before H2 in blog)
description: Multiple accessibility violations in heading hierarchy: (1) Footer uses H6 for 'Links' heading on all pages, skipping H4-H5 levels after H3/H2 content. Should use a semantically appropriate heading level or a non-heading element. (2) Blog post /posts/sample-post/ has H3 appearing before H2 in the content flow, breaking sequential heading order. WCAG 1.3.1 requires headings to convey document structure without skipping levels.

title: bug: missing skip-to-content link and main landmark on all pages
description: Accessibility issues found across all pages: (1) No skip-to-content/skip-navigation link exists anywhere on the site. Screen reader and keyboard users must tab through the entire navbar before reaching content. WCAG 2.4.1 requires a mechanism to bypass repeated content. (2) No <main> element or role=main landmark is used. The main content area is a <div class=grow> with no semantic role. Screen readers cannot identify the primary content region. (3) Auth pages (sign-in, sign-up, forgot-password) have zero ARIA attributes despite being interactive forms.

title: bug: 'Get Started' CTA on homepage links to /posts/ instead of sign-up
description: The primary call-to-action button 'Get Started' on the homepage hero section links to /posts/ (the blog listing page). For a SaaS landing page, 'Get Started' typically leads to the sign-up flow (/auth/sign-up). This may confuse users who click expecting to start using the product and instead see blog posts. Current: href=/posts/. Expected: href=/auth/sign-up or similar onboarding path.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: fixing Get Started CTA href

title: bug: GET /app (no trailing slash) returns 404 from static assets instead of auth redirect
description: GET /app falls through to Cloudflare static asset handler and returns Hugo 404 page. GET /app/ (with trailing slash) correctly triggers auth middleware and redirects to sign-in. Root cause: wrangler.toml run_worker_first includes '/app/*' but not '/app'. Fix: add '/app' to run_worker_first array.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work on adding /app to run_worker_first

title: bug: stale/empty session cookie prevents access to sign-in page (redirect loop)
description: handleGetSignIn and handleGetSignUp check for session cookie presence via hasSessionCookie(), which only verifies the cookie key exists without validating the value. With an expired or empty session cookie, users are redirected to / and cannot access sign-in. They get stuck in a loop between / and /auth/sign-in. Must manually clear cookies to recover. hasSessionCookie() in cookieBuilder.ts returns true for empty cookie values.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting work: investigating hasSessionCookie in cookieBuilder.ts

title: [quality] `Content-Length: 0` pass-through is undocumented and has no spec test
description:

`Content-Length: 0` pass-through is undocumented and has no spec test — the intentional behaviour is invisible.

Fix:
Add an inline comment explaining the deliberate pass-through, and add a test asserting a POST with `Content-Length: 0` is not blocked.

title: [quality] `makeAuthMock` is missing stubs for `requestPasswordReset` and `res...

Severity: High

Problem:
`makeAuthMock` is missing stubs for `requestPasswordReset` and `resetPassword` — the `as unknown as AuthInstance` cast silences the type error, hiding the gap.

Fix:
Add `requestPasswordReset: vi.fn()` and `resetPassword: vi.fn()` to the mock factory.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Fix already applied: makeAuthMock already contains requestPasswordReset and resetPassword stubs. All 67 tests pass.

title: [sp:09-review] Code review for ralph-automation
description: Review implemented code for quality, security, and adherence to spec requirements

title: Fix lock file race condition in ralph.sh
description:
**Code Review Finding**

- **Category**: quality
- **File**: ralph.sh:185-212
- **Severity**: Medium

## Description

The lock file check and creation are not atomic. A TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race condition exists between checking if the lock file exists and creating it.

## Risk

Two concurrent executions could both pass the check before either creates the lock, allowing concurrent runs despite the locking mechanism.

## Recommended Fix

Use `flock` or atomic file creation with `noclobber` option:
```bash
set -o noclobber
echo $$ > "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null || { echo "Lock exists"; exit 1; }
```

---

_Duplicate Key: ralph.sh:185:quality_
close_reason: Used atomic file creation with noclobber to prevent TOCTOU race condition

title: Improve PID validation in lock file check
description:
**Code Review Finding**

- **Category**: quality
- **File**: ralph.sh:179-181
- **Severity**: Medium

## Description

`kill -0 "$pid"` only checks if a process exists, not if it is actually a ralph.sh instance. PID reuse could lead to false positives.

## Risk

A stale lock file with a reused PID (now belonging to an unrelated process) would block legitimate execution of ralph.sh.

## Recommended Fix

Store process name or command in lock file and verify both PID and process identity:
```bash
is_ralph_process() {
    local pid="$1"
    kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && \
        [[ $(ps -p "$pid" -o comm= 2>/dev/null) == *ralph* ]]
}
```

---

_Duplicate Key: ralph.sh:179:quality_
close_reason: Added is_ralph_running() that validates both PID existence and process identity

title: Implement SHA256 checksum verification for downloaded binary
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-template-builder/spec.md §US-3, FR-020, data-model.md §Checksum Record
**Skills**: None (Bash script)
**Files**: scripts/install-tmplr.sh
**Acceptance**: 
- Embeds SHA256 checksums for all 4 platform binaries
- Uses sha256sum (Linux) or shasum -a 256 (macOS)
- Deletes file and exits if checksum mismatch
- Reports expected vs actual hash on failure
close_reason: Implemented SHA256 checksum verification with embedded checksums for all 4 platform binaries

title: Install binary to ~/.local/bin and make executable
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-template-builder/spec.md §US-3, FR-016, research.md §tmplr Installation Location
**Skills**: None (Bash script)
**Files**: scripts/install-tmplr.sh
**Acceptance**: 
- Creates ~/.local/bin if it does not exist
- Moves verified binary to ~/.local/bin/tmplr
- Makes binary executable (chmod +x)
- Skips download if already installed with correct version
close_reason: Installs to ~/.local/bin/tmplr, creates dir if needed, makes executable, skips if version matches

title: Add install-tmplr command to justfile
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-template-builder/spec.md §US-3
**Skills**: None (justfile)
**Files**: justfile
**Acceptance**: 
- just install-tmplr command exists
- Invokes scripts/install-tmplr.sh

close_reason: Added install-tmplr command to justfile in earlier commit

title: [sp:09-review] Code review for template-builder
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-template-builder/spec.md, plan.md
**Skills**: /code-review, /security-review
**Context**: Review implemented code against spec requirements
**Acceptance**: No blocking issues, ready for merge

comments.author: sprite
comments.text: Starting code review for template-builder. Reviewing build-template.sh and install-tmplr.sh against spec requirements.

comments.author: sprite
comments.text:
Code review complete. Found 4 issues:

**HIGH - Bug (clawtask-4qj.1.1):** is_excluded() false positive - prefix match on '.git' without trailing slash incorrectly excludes .github/, .gitignore, .gitattributes from template output.

**MEDIUM - Bug (clawtask-4qj.1.2):** files_excluded counter runs in subshell pipe, always reports 0 in summary output.

**LOW - Spec deviation (clawtask-4qj.1.3):** Output filename is dist/turtlebased-ts.tmplr.gz but spec FR-002 requires dist/template.tmplr. Compression also not in spec.

**LOW - Scope creep (clawtask-4qj.1.4):** wrangler.worker.toml, cron handler, and scaffold dirs added but not in spec. FR-014 comment references are incorrect.

**Positive observations:** Good use of set -euo pipefail, cleanup traps, SHA256 verification, platform detection, version pinning. install-tmplr.sh is solid. Code is well-organized and documented.

No security vulnerabilities found. The HIGH bug (exclusion logic) should be fixed before merge as it would produce broken templates missing critical files.
